reviews.review_description:
This game really shines when you take the extra step to print out the physical pages to the manual which really helps add to the frantic fun and just really takes it to another level...do yourself a favor and familiarize yourself with the game and how it works ahead of time for more seamless fun and less confusion...

My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed opportunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I don't see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I received a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.
Awesome game to play with friends! Just needs gamepad controller support like it does on pc and it would be perfect.
Hello. I have czech manual 058 and in wires that go from number to letter I have instruction only to number 9 but there are up to 12 in bomb.
I never had a chance to play
Pulled it out for a party. Kind of a bust. Most people were newer to vr and really struggled. It should be setup for like 8 minutes by default not 5.
VR game developers should make more games like this, with less moving around and more observing, thinking, and interacting.

super fun, we definitely will play more. the idea of one headset for a group of people is brilliant. more of this type of games, please! (navigating a haunted house or some kind of bewitched forest etc)

Such a great game, especially with the family.  It really encourages team work, good communication, and a willingness to learn how to delegate tasks out for best efficiency.  I play this with my 3 kids, ages from elementary school to high school.  Mom approves because we all take turns and the kids aren’t inside the headset for more than 5 minutes at a time.  

We’re still making our way thru all the levels, but I can already tell I want more.  Here’s my wishlist:

1. More modules!  Release as a DLC pack and I’m happy to pay more for it.  The cost-to-entertainment value is already superb for what you get.

2. Tools!  There’s an empty drawer in the virtual desk.  Fill it with tools and let us use them.  Keeping tools organized, figuring out which to use, accidentally dropping one on floor, etc, will all be amazing adds for the game. Would love some wire cutters for the wire module.  Maybe some screwdrivers to take components out. A boroscope to look inside the bomb.  A multimeter to check voltages, continuity, etc.  Endless opportunities here!

3.  Give option for passthrough mode while diffusing the bomb.  I get that the virtual room provides the atmosphere and fun distractions, but passthrough for certain players would be much appreciated.    

4.  As others have mentioned, adjustable height for the table is needed. 

5.  The first time the timer ran out on me, I had all this anxiety not knowing what to expect, and was then underwhelmed with the explosion. More sound, maybe a flash of light, etc, is needed to really make it a “wow!” moment.  

6. through 10.  Did I mention more modules? ;)

Already a great game as is.  This should be an instant purchase for anyone who likes party games.  Just do it.

reviews.review_title: Great game experience. Some small issues
reviews.review_description:
The overall game experience is great, it is what you get on a regular desktop, plus the immersive and mobile experience with the Quest. The only usability issue I found was that I couldn't adjust the height of the table (or the height of the avatar), so the table was too high if I was sitting on my couch. A workaround was to adjust the floor level on the Quest settings. Some usability improvements would make the game more comfortable to play. Wearing a VR headset is already tiresome enough for most people.

Some suggestions:
* Shortcut to adjust the in-game avatar height or the floor level
* Offer an option to toggle the virtual table off, and have a transparent surface to put the bomb and clock, something that would work on any kind of couch or chair you are sitting or lying on.
* Accessibility mode with zero gravity, so the bomb stays in place if you drop it.

If there is still development bandwidth to drop some extra love into this game, it would be much appreciated.

By the way, very fun game! Highly recommended.

Originally a Steam game, Keep Talking and Nobody Explodes for VR is a perfect translation to VR. It makes defusing bombs a much more tactile experience, and you feel much more immersed in the environment. The MR mode on the quest 3 is a nice addition too, allowing you to see the friends you are playing with. More challenging levels become much more stressful for everyone, especially the person defusing the bomb, but hey, defusing bombs has always been a bit stressful!
I have this game on steam and it is good fun, but playing it in VR is even better.

reviews.review_title: Decent party game but only on sale
reviews.review_description: This is an ok party game for a group of at least 3, but with just 2 people it becomes boring rather quickly.  The price is also too high for what you get.  At $10 this would be 3 stars and at $5 it would be 4 stars.
It's a really fun game but it definitely needs an online multiplayer mode where 1 person has the bomb and the other person can view the manual in vr

It’s super fun to play, and really tests your communication skills! I had an absolute blast playing with my family, and it was super smooth, without any lag! It was really hard, and is definitely frustrating at times, but that’s all part of the fun!! 

10/10 game, totally worth it!
You have to have friends 💀
Make it two play through vr
I don’t have friends and I thought it would be an online experience but no so I can’t even play the game

Every time I finish a bomb in seated mode the binder is exactly beneath me on the floor and I simply cannot go to the next level. I tried everything I could think of and it's not getting better. I would love to be able to get my money back.

reviews.review_title: Very fun to play with siblings
reviews.review_description: If your family just got an oculus, this is a great game for local multiplayer!

This has kind of the same vibe as an escape room to me.  More than one person is required; one wearing the headset looking at the bomb and one or more reading the bomb defusal manual.  I had to enter the code from the defusal manual on the first play, so hopefully there will be more manuals in the future that will extend the life of this game.  The bomb is different each round so that helps with replay value.  We have only played this one night so far, but it was a lot of fun. We experienced a glitch of the bomb manual getting stuck in the table which was kind of frustrating and it wasn't initially clear how to actually start a defusing session, but we figured it out.

I was looking forward to this as a fun game with a small group. But I didn't realize that the instructions to defuse the bomb are a static download, meaning they are ALWAYS THE SAME. Aside from a few possible options for defusing each bomb module, this game get's old QUICKLY. Worst of all, is that Oculus claimed I could get a refund if I haven't played for more than 2 hours, yet I've been trying to find a way to request a refund and just keep going in circles with their support page....

The game was originally released in 2015. It has many officially supported languages, including Polish. I have printed the Polish official manual from the official page. When I bought the app I discovered that there is no language choice, only English! This is a major flaw and I do not understand it. The translations are ready, there is no work to be done - Steam version officially supports numerous languages. Oculus version does not. This game is a party game and language support is crucial. Please consider this and add another languages to the game.

developer_response.body: Hello there. Translations for the game have been added for 26 new languages including Polish. To change languages, please open the dossier menu and select "Language".

reviews.review_title: Exactly what it says it is
reviews.review_description: This game is amazing for parties and can really work with almost anyone. It is really fun to have to communicate with others about something that they know and you don’t and that they have to explain to you. It is also super entertaining to watch others try to test their communication skills and see how fast they can defuse the bomb!
I love this game even more than I do on the PC! The graphics are simple so they work great on the Quest, and it feels even more stressful! Great if you have someone to play with often.
Won’t download on my oculus quest!!!!!

developer_response.body: Hello cortkaufmann. This sounds like an issue that may require Oculus's help to fix. But before doing that, we'd love to help try to get your game running as it should. Please email us at support@keeptalkinggame.com and we'll get back to you shortly.

reviews.review_title: Paid without imformation of a headset
reviews.review_description: After I paid for it, it said can't install without headset after I paid for it which sucks

Don’t miss out on this one!

This is a puzzle game with a TON of variety. Each time you play, you will get a little bit more comfortable either working the bomb or reading the manual, and I find it equally challenging and satisfying completing both tasks. It takes a lot of practice (and patience) to do well, but nothing beats celebrating your victory by Gronk-spiking a diffused bomb!

I would highly recommend for the manual players to bring some scratch paper, as some of the puzzles are hard to keep track of without writing some notes. Also, even though you can’t see the bomb, listen for audio cues to help your diffuser multi-task. 

Its marketed as a party-style game, but I bought it mostly to play with my wife. That actually makes it easier to get thru the harder puzzles because we’ve practiced together a lot. 

I really can’t say enough good things about this game! Super impressed!
There really isn't anything like this game. It's a great way of using VR's exclusive nature as a tool for multiplayer fun.
A cool game to test your teamwork skills.
This game is worth every cent. There’s tons of different bombs to choose from, and the free play mode offers tons of replay-ability.

This is a great game for local get together as with friends and family and i don’t k or if it will be too much or just too hard but i think being able to make a party with a friend and give them a digital manual to look at or somehow make a co-op bomb. Again a really fun game i just wish i could play with people that are just next to me.

What a fun game. My wife and I play for a few minutes every night. Great way to argue with your spouse and threaten to divorce if the bomb blows up! 😂😂. Seriously, we both enjoy playing this game together.

I usually don’t take to first impressions, especially when it comes to games. However, the great detail and quirky idiosyncrasies of the game make it all the better. I was so impressed by this game that after playing it for 45 minutes, I have decided to gift it to a friend. This game is great for just two people, or a family game night or get together. Every aspect is so well thought out, so it is also aesthetically pleasing to anyone who is a sucker for detail. The game play itself is flawless, definitely one of the most memorable experiences of Oculus. I would recommend it in an instant to anyone considering to purchase it.
I’ve only been playing for a day but I can’t stop thinking about it.  So well done. I watched a couple videos and there are different modules out there. Will those come to quest as well?
For a bit of fun with your friends and family, i would highly recommend.
This game helps you bond with your friends and family
This is one of my favorite games. I can play with my kids and they really enjoy it too. Minus 1 star because the table physics are off and the book falls through. Otherwise it’s challenging and fun.
Recently bought this and spent 3 of us spent over 2hrs taking it in turns to be the bomb diffuser and experts. Really good fun! (Be prepared for the odd argument lol).
Cool game in coop but it needs to do some improvements like to see our hands instead of a controller. Having some tools like scissors to cut the wires will be great too.
